_about: spck.dev UCP conformance coverage — every normative MUST accounted as CHECK (kill-rate-validated), EXEMPT (documented), or GAP. Generated by conformance/coverage/matrix.py --json; the `coverage` CI gate fails if this file is stale or coverage regresses.

rows.requirement: Create Checkout initiates a new checkout session via POST /checkout-sessions
Get Checkout retrieves current state via GET /checkout-sessions/{id}
Update Checkout updates a session via PUT /checkout-sessions/{id}
Complete Checkout places the order via POST /checkout-sessions/{id}/complete
Cancel Checkout cancels a session via POST /checkout-sessions/{id}/cancel
Update Checkout performs a full replacement of the checkout resource; the platform is REQUIRED to send the entire checkout resource
Each PUT replaces the entire session, so clients must include all previously set fields they wish to retain
Complete Checkout response is the checkout object with the order field populated (order provides id and permalink_url)
After a checkout session reaches the state completed it is considered immutable
Status field enum is constrained to the six lifecycle values
Checkout response MUST include required top-level fields: ucp, id, line_items, status, currency, totals, links
Checkout status indicates the current phase and required action; the business sets the status
ucp envelope field is omitted on request bodies (ucp_request: omit)
status, totals, messages, links, expires_at, continue_url, and order are response-only (ucp_request: omit) and omitted on requests
Businesses MUST provide continue_url when returning status = requires_escalation (Availability)
continue_url MUST be an absolute HTTPS URL
Platform MUST use continue_url when checkout status is requires_escalation (Platform guideline)
Business MUST send a confirmation email after the checkout has been completed
Logic handling the checkout sessions MUST be deterministic
Business MUST provide continue_url when returning status = requires_escalation (Business guideline restatement)
Business MUST include at least one message with severity: escalation when returning status = requires_escalation
All REST request and response bodies MUST be valid JSON per RFC 8259
All REST endpoints MUST be served over HTTPS with minimum TLS version 1.3
All requests MUST include the UCP-Agent header containing the platform profile URI (RFC 8941 dictionary syntax)
All capability and service names MUST use the format {reverse-domain}.{service}.{capability}
The spec and schema fields are REQUIRED for all capabilities
The origin of capability spec/schema URLs MUST match the namespace authority
Platform MUST validate the spec-origin/namespace binding and SHOULD reject capabilities where the spec origin does not match the namespace authority
Vendors MUST use their own reverse-domain namespace for custom capabilities
Transport definitions MUST be thin: they declare method names and reference base schemas only
A service endpoint MUST be a valid URL with scheme (https)
Transport definitions MUST NOT enumerate fields or define payload shapes inline
Each extension schema MUST declare the types it introduces and how it modifies base types using allOf composition
Platforms MUST resolve schemas client-side by fetching and composing base schemas with active extension schemas
Discovery profile at /.well-known/ucp MUST return 200 OK with the expected version, capabilities, and payment handlers
All spec and schema URLs advertised in the discovery profile MUST be valid and resolvable (return 200 with valid HTML/JSON)
Submitting discounts.codes replaces any previously submitted codes (replacement semantics).
Sending an empty codes array clears all discount codes.
Discount codes are matched case-insensitively by the business.
discounts.applied contains all active discounts (code-based and automatic) in the response.
When multiple valid discount codes are submitted, all are applied (accept-both) and appear in discounts.applied.
When a submitted discount code cannot be applied, the business communicates this via the messages[] array.
A rejected discount code still appears in discounts.codes (echoed back) but not in discounts.applied.
Automatic discounts appear in discounts.applied with automatic: true and no code field; they are applied without platform action and cannot be removed by the platform.
A fixed-amount discount code decreases the total correctly and surfaces applied discount details (code and amount).
An applied_discount must include title and amount; amount is a non-negative integer in minor currency units.
applied_discount.method, when present, must be one of 'each' or 'across'.
An allocation must include path and amount; allocation amount is a non-negative integer in minor currency units.
The sum of allocations[].amount must equal the applied_discount.amount.
All discount amounts are positive integers in minor currency units; when presenting totals, discount types are displayed as subtractive.
totals[type=items_discount].amount must equal sum(line_items[].discount).
Buyer consent is communicated as boolean states within the buyer.consent object (analytics, preferences, marketing, sale_of_data) on create_checkout and update_checkout.
Each buyer consent state (analytics, preferences, marketing, sale_of_data), when present, must be a boolean.
Consent is declarative: the protocol communicates consent but does not enforce it, and legal compliance remains the business's responsibility.
An error message MUST include the fields type, code, content, and severity (all four are required on a Message Error).
The error message type discriminator MUST be the constant string "error".

The error code field MUST be a string; defined values include missing, invalid, out_of_stock, payment_declined, requires_sign_in, requires_3ds, requires_identity_linking, with freeform codes also allowed.
The error severity field MUST be one of exactly three enum values: recoverable, requires_buyer_input, requires_buyer_review.
Errors with requires_* severity contribute to status requires_escalation.
REST implementations MUST use standard HTTP status codes (e.g., 200, 201, 400, 401, 500) for responses including errors.
If negotiation fails, businesses MUST return an error response (status requires_escalation with a type:error message, e.g. version_unsupported, severity requires_buyer_input).
Businesses MUST include at least one message with severity escalation when returning status requires_escalation.
A fulfillment_method MUST include id, type, and line_item_ids
fulfillment_method.type MUST be one of shipping or pickup
A fulfillment_group MUST include id and line_item_ids
A fulfillment_option MUST include id, title, and totals
options[].title is the required primary label that distinguishes the option from its siblings
options[].title MUST distinguish this option from its siblings
options[].title MUST be sufficient for buyer decision if description is absent
options[].description MUST NOT repeat title or total; provides supplementary context only
options[].total is the required price in minor units and may be null if not yet available
available_methods[].description MUST be a standalone sentence explaining what, when, and where
When supports_multi_group is false (default), the business MUST consolidate all items into a single group per method
Extensions that add new fulfillment method types MUST add an extension schema that adds the method to the fulfillment_method type enum and adds corresponding business config options
A shipping_destination MUST include an id (it is a postal_address plus a required id)
Platforms must authenticate using their client_id and client_secret through HTTP Basic Authentication when exchanging codes for tokens.
Platforms must implement the OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code flow (RFC 6749 4.1) as the primary linking mechanism.
Businesses must implement OAuth 2.0 (RFC 6749).
Businesses must adhere to RFC 8414 to declare the location of their OAuth 2.0 endpoints at /.well-known/oauth-authorization-server.
Businesses must enforce Client Authentication at the Token Endpoint.
Businesses must provide an account creation flow if the user does not already have an account.
Businesses must support standard UCP scopes, granting tokens permission to all associated Operations for a given resource.
Businesses must implement standard Token Revocation as defined in RFC 7009.
Businesses must revoke the specified token and should recursively revoke all associated tokens (revoking a refresh_token must also immediately revoke all access_tokens issued from it).
Businesses must support revocation requests authenticated with the same client credentials used for the token endpoint.
A scope covering a capability must grant access to all operations associated with that capability (e.g., ucp:scopes:checkout_session must grant Get, Create, Update, Delete, Cancel, Complete).
When an Idempotency-Key is provided, the server MUST store the key with the operation result for at least 24 hours
When an Idempotency-Key is provided, the server MUST return the cached result for duplicate keys
When an Idempotency-Key is provided, the server MUST return 409 Conflict if the key is reused with different parameters
409 Conflict indicates the request could not be completed due to a conflict (e.g., idempotent key reuse)
Platforms MUST communicate their profile URI with each request to enable capability negotiation
For HTTP transport, platforms MUST use Dictionary Structured Field syntax (RFC 8941) in the UCP-Agent header
Platforms MUST include their profile URI in every request using the transport-appropriate mechanism
Platforms MUST validate that capability spec URI origins match namespace authorities
Platforms MUST fetch and compose schemas for negotiated capabilities before making requests
Upon receiving a request with a platform profile URI, businesses MUST fetch and validate the platform profile unless already cached
Businesses MUST compute the intersection of platform and business capabilities
Extensions without their parent capability in the intersection MUST be excluded
Businesses MUST include the ucp field in every response containing version and active capabilities
If negotiation fails, businesses MUST return an error response
Businesses MUST validate the platform's version and determine compatibility
If platform version <= business version, the business MUST process the request
If platform version > business version, the business MUST return version_unsupported error
Businesses MUST include the version used for processing in every response
All UCP communication MUST occur over HTTPS
REST requests and responses MUST use Content-Type application/json
REST implementations MUST use standard HTTP verbs (e.g., POST for creation, GET for retrieval)
REST implementations MUST use standard HTTP status codes (e.g., 200, 201, 400, 401, 500)

Get Order MUST return the full order entity as a current-state snapshot (complete record of what was purchased, how it will be delivered, and what has happened since placement), reflecting line items, fulfillment, and event logs
The order entity MUST include the required top-level fields ucp, id, checkout_id, permalink_url, line_items, fulfillment, and totals
currency MUST NOT be treated as a required top-level order field in spec version 2026-01-23 (currency is absent from order.json required[])
Each order line item MUST include id, item, quantity, totals, and status
Line item quantity MUST include both total and fulfilled counts
Line items reflect what was purchased at checkout and MUST be retained as the immutable source of truth for what was ordered
Adjustments are an append-only log; each adjustment MUST include id, type, occurred_at, and status
Each fulfillment event (append-only log of actual shipments) MUST include id, occurred_at, type, and line_items
Each fulfillment expectation (buyer-facing grouping) MUST include id, line_items, method_type, and destination
The platform MUST provide its webhook URL (webhook_url) in the order capability's config field during negotiation; the platform order capability config requires webhook_url
Businesses POST order lifecycle events to the platform-provided webhook URL, sending the full order entity (not incremental deltas)
Businesses MUST send an 'Order created' event with a fully populated order entity
Webhook payloads MUST be signed by the business and verified by the platform to ensure authenticity and integrity

Businesses MUST sign all webhook payloads using a key from their signing_keys array (published in /.well-known/ucp), and the signature MUST be included in the Request-Signature header as a detached JWT (RFC 7797)
Businesses MUST retry failed webhook deliveries
Businesses MUST include a business identifier in the webhook path or headers
The platform MUST respond quickly with a 2xx HTTP status code to acknowledge receipt of a webhook, then process events asynchronously after responding

On webhook verification: the platform extracts Request-Signature, parses the JWT header for kid, fetches the business UCP profile, locates the matching key in signing_keys, and verifies the signature against the request body; if verification fails, it MUST reject the webhook with an appropriate error response
Every payment handler specification must define the core required elements.
A payment handler base/declaration must include an id field identifying the handler instance.
Payment handler specifications must explicitly document the participant field mappings.
A payment handler specification must define which credential types are accepted by the handler.
If using token credentials, the handler schema must include an expiry/ttl field (or mechanism to refresh credentials).
A handler specification must define a mapping for common payment failures.
A payment instrument must include id, handler_id, and type, where handler_id corresponds to the id of the payment handler definition.
Credentials flow Platform to Business only; businesses must not echo credentials back in responses (unidirectional flow).
The handler_id in the payload ensures the business knows which payment credential provider key to use, preventing key confusion attacks (Handler ID Routing).
Businesses must filter the handlers list based on the context of the cart (dynamic filtering).
The card credential type must not be used for checkout, only with payment handlers that tokenize or encrypt credentials.
Both parties handling a CardCredential (sender and receiver) must be PCI DSS compliant, and transmission must use HTTPS/TLS with strong cipher suites.
A card credential must include type (const 'card') and card_number_type.
A token credential must include type and token.
A binding must include checkout_id identifying the checkout session the token is bound to (prevents token reuse across checkouts/participants).
The binding identity is required when acting on behalf of another participant (e.g., agent tokenizing for merchant).
Businesses must embed a cryptographic signature (merchant_authorization) in checkout responses when AP2 is negotiated.
Platforms must provide cryptographically signed proofs (Mandates) for autonomous/non-repudiable authorization.
When ap2_mandate is negotiated, the business must include ap2.merchant_authorization in all relevant checkout responses.
When ap2_mandate is negotiated, the business must not accept a complete_checkout request that lacks ap2.checkout_mandate.
The platform must verify the business's merchant signature before presenting checkout terms to the user.
A public signing key must be available for the business to verify the mandate's signature.
If a public key cannot be resolved, or if the signature is invalid, the business must return an error.
All AP2 signatures must use one of the approved algorithms (ES256/ES384/ES512).
Businesses must embed their signature in the checkout response body under ap2.merchant_authorization.
The AP2 signature must cover both the JWS header and the checkout payload.
Completion of an autonomous AP2 flow must produce two distinct mandate artifacts: a checkout_mandate and a payment_mandate.
The checkout mandate must contain the full checkout response including the business's signature (nested signature model).
Both parties must follow the locked AP2 completion flow once the ap2_mandate capability is negotiated; a completion request without mandates must result in a session failure.
A completion request without mandates must result in a session failure.
For signature computation over JSON payloads, implementations must use JSON canonicalization (JCS).
On receiving the complete request, the business must check the mandate is present and return a mandate_required error if ap2.checkout_mandate is missing.
The merchant_authorization is a JWS Detached Content signature; its header must contain alg (ES256/ES384/ES512) and kid claims.
A request to the simulation endpoint without the Simulation-Secret header MUST be rejected with 403 Forbidden.
A request to the simulation endpoint with an incorrect Simulation-Secret MUST be rejected with 403 Forbidden.
A request to the simulation endpoint with the correct Simulation-Secret MUST be accepted with 200 OK.
All UCP communication MUST occur over HTTPS.
When an out-of-stock item is added to a checkout session, the server MUST return a 400 Bad Request error indicating insufficient stock.
An inventory/quantity validation failure on checkout creation MUST return a 400 Bad Request error.
A request referencing a non-existent product MUST return a 400 Bad Request error indicating the product was not found.
Submitting a payment instrument with a known failing token at checkout completion MUST return a 402 Payment Required error.
Attempting to complete a checkout without required fulfillment MUST return a 400 Bad Request error.
Error responses MUST be structured: a 400 error body MUST contain a populated 'detail' field describing the error.
An update specifying an invalid adjustment status (non-conforming literal) MUST be rejected with a 422 Unprocessable Entity error due to validation failure.
An update with a malformed adjustments field (e.g., a dict instead of a list) MUST return a 422 Unprocessable Entity error.
A request using an unknown discount code MUST succeed with 200 OK, with no discount applied (graceful handling, not an error).

line_items is required on create and update requests and omitted on complete requests
currency is omitted on create, update, and complete requests (merchant-determined, response-only)
status, totals, messages, links, expires_at, continue_url, and order are response-only (ucp_request: omit) and omitted on requests
payment is optional on create and update, and required on complete requests

The buyer consent object is optional on create and update operations and must be omitted on complete (consent is not required to gate completion).

Error responses MUST be structured: a 400 error body MUST contain a populated 'detail' field describing the error.
An update specifying an invalid adjustment status (non-conforming literal) MUST be rejected with a 422 Unprocessable Entity error due to validation failure.
An update with a malformed adjustments field (e.g., a dict instead of a list) MUST return a 422 Unprocessable Entity error.
A request using an unknown discount code MUST succeed with 200 OK, with no discount applied (graceful handling, not an error).
On cart-to-checkout conversion, business MUST use cart contents and MUST ignore overlapping fields in the checkout payload
If an incomplete checkout already exists for the given cart_id, business MUST return the existing checkout session rather than creating a new one (idempotent conversion)
For Update Cart (full replacement), the platform MUST send the entire cart resource
On Cancel Cart, business MUST return the cart state before deletion
Signal values MUST NOT be buyer-asserted claims
All cart REST request and response bodies MUST be valid JSON as specified in RFC 8259
All cart REST endpoints MUST be served over HTTPS with minimum TLS version 1.3
All cart requests MUST include the UCP-Agent header containing the platform profile URI

When an Idempotency-Key is provided, the server MUST store the key with the operation result for at least 24 hours, return the cached result for duplicate keys, and return 409 Conflict if the key is reused with different parameters
A cart response object MUST include ucp, id, line_items, currency, and totals
cart_id is only available on create_checkout (create:optional) and MUST be omitted on update
Each cart line item MUST include id, item, quantity, and totals
Line item quantity MUST be an integer of at least 1
Each line item's item MUST include id, title, and price
Pagination response MUST include has_next_page
When has_next_page is true, cursor MUST be present (if/then schema constraint)
Cursor to fetch next page MUST be present when has_next_page is true
limit request parameter has minimum 1 and default 10
Clients MUST NOT assume the response size equals the requested limit
A valid search request MUST include at least one of: a query string, one or more filters, or an extension-defined input
Implementations MUST validate that incoming requests contain at least one recognized input
Empty search returns products:[] with no messages (not an error)
Lookup MUST support lookup by product ID and variant ID
get_product MUST support product ID and variant ID for the single id parameter
Duplicate identifiers in the request MUST be deduplicated
When multiple identifiers resolve to the same product, it MUST be returned once
Variants without an inputs entry MUST NOT appear in lookup responses
lookup response variants MUST include a non-empty inputs array (correlation metadata)
For get_product / catalog/product, an unknown identifier (NOT_FOUND) returns HTTP 200 with ucp.status:"error" and a descriptive message
All application-level outcomes (incl. not-found) return HTTP 200 with the UCP envelope and optional messages array
When the Lookup capability is advertised, both /catalog/lookup and /catalog/product MUST be available
REST transport MUST implement endpoints for each advertised catalog capability per its requirements
REST transport MUST support cursor-based pagination with default limit of 10
REST transport MUST return HTTP 200 for lookup requests; unknown identifiers result in fewer products returned
REST transport MUST return HTTP 400 with request_too_large error for requests exceeding batch size limits
Lookup implementations MAY enforce a maximum batch size and MUST reject requests exceeding their limit with an appropriate error
Lookup ids array is required with minItems 1
search_response MUST include ucp and products
lookup_response MUST include ucp and products
get_product_response MUST include ucp and a singular product object
For Variant ID lookups via get_product, the requested variant MUST be the first element (featured)
get_product response MUST include product.selected when the product has configurable options
In get_product selected input, each option name MUST appear at most once
Eligibility and policy enforcement MUST occur at checkout time using binding transaction data (catalog context signals are provisional)
Signal values MUST NOT be buyer-asserted claims
Disclosure warnings (presentation:"disclosure") carry notices that platforms must not hide or dismiss
continue_url MUST be provided when status is requires_escalation (Availability section)
continue_url MUST be an absolute HTTPS URL
Platform MUST use continue_url when checkout status is requires_escalation (Platform guideline)
Business MUST send a confirmation email after the checkout has been completed
Logic handling the checkout sessions MUST be deterministic
Business MUST provide continue_url when returning status = requires_escalation (Business guideline restatement)
Business MUST include at least one message with severity requires_buyer_input or requires_buyer_review when returning status = requires_escalation
After a checkout session reaches state completed it is considered immutable
Create Checkout initiates a new checkout session via POST /checkout-sessions
Get Checkout retrieves current state via GET /checkout-sessions/{id}
Update Checkout updates a session via PUT /checkout-sessions/{id}
Complete Checkout places the order via POST /checkout-sessions/{id}/complete
Cancel Checkout cancels a session via POST /checkout-sessions/{id}/cancel
Update Checkout performs a full replacement of the checkout resource; platform is REQUIRED to send the entire checkout resource
Each PUT replaces the entire session; clients must include all previously set fields they wish to retain
Complete Checkout response is the checkout object with the order field populated
status field enum is constrained to the six lifecycle values
Checkout response MUST include required top-level fields: ucp, id, line_items, status, currency, totals, links
Top-level checkout id is omitted on request bodies (ucp_request: omit)
ucp envelope field is omitted on request bodies (ucp_request: omit)
status, currency, totals, messages, links, expires_at, continue_url, order are response-only (ucp_request: omit) and omitted on requests
line_items is required on create and update requests and omitted on complete requests
payment is optional on create and update, and required on complete requests
continue_url MUST be provided when status is requires_escalation (schema field description)
All REST request and response bodies MUST be valid JSON per RFC 8259
All REST endpoints MUST be served over HTTPS with minimum TLS version 1.3
All requests MUST include the UCP-Agent header containing the platform profile URI (RFC 8941 dictionary syntax)
When an Idempotency-Key is provided, server MUST store the key+result >=24h, return cached result for duplicates, and return 409 if reused with different parameters
Protocol errors return appropriate HTTP status (401/403/409/429/503) with JSON body containing code and content
Business outcomes (including unavailable merchandise) return HTTP 200 with the UCP envelope and messages array
On create with all items unavailable, response carries ucp.status:error with messages and no resource body
Platforms MUST NOT autonomously complete a checkout with mismatched totals; SHOULD reject or escalate for buyer review via continue_url
Businesses MUST NOT complete a transaction with unresolved eligibility claims; accepted claims MUST be resolved before completion
Business profile MUST be served over HTTPS.
Profile endpoints MUST NOT use 3xx redirects; verifiers MUST NOT follow them.
Profile responses MUST send Cache-Control with public and max-age>=60, and MUST NOT use private/no-store/no-cache.
Implementations MUST reject profile URLs not served over HTTPS.
service endpoint MUST be a valid HTTPS URL.
Platforms MUST include their profile URI in every request.
Submitting discounts.codes replaces any previously submitted codes (replacement semantics)
Sending an empty array codes:[] removes all discount codes
Discount codes are matched case-insensitively by the business
discounts.applied contains all active discounts (code-based + automatic)
Rejected codes are communicated via the messages[] array
Discount amounts are reflected in totals[] and line_items[].totals[]
When a cart is converted to a checkout via cart_id, businesses MUST carry forward any discount codes that were applied to the cart
Automatic discounts appear in discounts.applied with automatic:true and no code field
Automatic discounts cannot be removed by the platform
When the discount extension is active, businesses that accept eligibility claims MUST surface their effect on pricing as provisional discounts in the applied array
Platforms MUST display provisional discounts to the buyer
When a business recognizes an eligibility claim that affects pricing, it MUST surface a corresponding provisional discount in discounts.applied
At checkout completion, all remaining provisional claims MUST be resolved
If a discount has allocations pointing to line items it contributes to items_discount; otherwise (no allocations, or shipping/fees allocations) it contributes to discount
Invariant: totals[type=items_discount].amount equals sum of line_items[].totals[type=items_discount].amount
Discount amounts in discounts.applied are positive integers (value of the discount)
Discount entries in totals[] are negative (strictly < 0); the sign is schema-enforced
Invariant: sum of allocations[].amount equals applied_discount.amount
applied_discount MUST include title and amount
applied_discount.method MUST be one of 'each' or 'across' when present
applied_discount.priority MUST be an integer >= 1 (lower applied first)
allocation MUST include path and amount
discounts.applied is response-only and MUST be omitted from requests
On checkout complete, the discounts object MUST be omitted from the request
Multiple valid discount codes are both applied (accept-both)
When multiple codes are submitted and one is invalid, only the valid discount is applied (accept-one-reject-one); rejected appears in messages[] not applied
When the consent extension is supported, the buyer object in checkout is extended with a consent field containing boolean consent states
consent properties (analytics, preferences, marketing, sale_of_data) MUST be booleans when present
On checkout complete, the buyer object (carrying consent) MUST be omitted from the request
Error message MUST include type, code, content, and severity fields.
Error message type discriminator MUST be the constant "error".
Error message severity MUST be one of recoverable, requires_buyer_input, requires_buyer_review, unrecoverable.
Errors with requires_* severity contribute to status: requires_escalation.
Error message content_type, when present, MUST be one of plain or markdown (default plain).
Error message path, when present, MUST be an RFC 9535 JSONPath to the referenced component.
Warning message MUST include type, code, and content fields.
Warning message type discriminator MUST be the constant "warning".
Warning content is a human-readable message that MUST be displayed.
Warning presentation is the rendering contract; 'notice' (default) platform MUST display, MAY dismiss; 'disclosure' platform MUST display in proximity to path, MUST NOT hide or auto-dismiss.
For all warnings, platforms MUST display the warning content to the buyer (both notice and disclosure).
For disclosure warnings, platforms MUST display the warning content to the buyer.
For disclosure warnings, platforms MUST display the warning in proximity to the path-referenced component; when path is omitted the disclosure applies to the response as a whole.
For disclosure warnings, platforms MUST NOT hide, collapse, or auto-dismiss the warning.
For disclosure warnings, platforms MUST render image_url when present.
Platforms that cannot honor the disclosure rendering contract MUST escalate to merchant UI via continue_url rather than silently downgrading to a notice.
For notice warnings, platforms MUST display the warning content to the buyer.
Businesses MUST set presentation: disclosure when warning content must be displayed alongside a specific component and must not be hidden or auto-dismissed.
Info message MUST include type and content fields.
Info message type discriminator MUST be the constant "info".
Error response MUST include ucp and messages.
Error response ucp.status MUST be 'error'.
Error response messages array MUST contain at least one message (minItems 1).
A Message MUST be exactly one of error, warning, or info (oneOf discriminated container).
ucp.status is the shape discriminator: 'success' carries the expected payload, 'error' carries error information instead.
A fulfillment_method MUST include id, type, and line_item_ids
fulfillment_method.type MUST be one of shipping or pickup
A fulfillment_group MUST include id and line_item_ids
A fulfillment_option MUST include id, title, and totals
options[].title is the required primary label that distinguishes the option from its siblings
options[].title MUST distinguish this option from its siblings
options[].title MUST be sufficient for buyer decision if description is absent
options[].description MUST NOT repeat title or total; provides supplementary context only
options[].total is the required price in minor units and may be null if not yet available
available_methods[].description MUST be a standalone sentence explaining what, when, and where
When supports_multi_group is false (default), the business MUST consolidate all items into a single group per method
Extensions that add new fulfillment method types MUST add an extension schema that adds the method to the fulfillment_method type enum and adds corresponding business config options
A shipping_destination MUST include an id (it is a postal_address plus a required id)
Identity linking uses OAuth 2.0 as the v1 auth mechanism.
Platforms MUST authenticate token-endpoint requests using a method advertised in the business's token_endpoint_auth_methods_supported metadata.
Public clients MUST use token-endpoint auth method 'none' and rely on PKCE with S256 as proof-of-possession of the authorization code.
Public clients MUST NOT embed a client_secret.
Platforms MUST select the strongest token-endpoint auth method offered by the business compatible with the platform's deployment model.
Platforms MUST include user identity tokens in the HTTP Authorization header using the Bearer scheme.
Platforms MUST process WWW-Authenticate: Bearer challenges on 401 and 403 responses to user-authenticated operations.
Platforms MUST extract the scope parameter (when present) from the WWW-Authenticate challenge to construct subsequent authorization requests.
Platforms MUST implement the OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code flow as the account linking mechanism.
Platforms MUST use PKCE with code_challenge_method=S256 for all authorization code exchanges.

Platforms MUST validate the iss parameter in the authorization response to prevent Mix-Up Attacks, verifying it matches the AS issuer URI; if it does not match they MUST abort and discard the authorization response.
Platforms MUST call the business's token revocation endpoint when a user initiates an unlink action on the platform side.
Businesses MUST implement OAuth 2.0.
Businesses MUST publish authorization server metadata via RFC 8414 at /.well-known/oauth-authorization-server.
Businesses MUST populate scopes_supported in RFC 8414 metadata so platforms can detect scope mismatches before initiating an authorization flow.
Businesses MUST return the iss parameter in the authorization response.
Businesses MUST enforce PKCE validation at the token endpoint for all authorization code exchanges; requests without a valid code_verifier MUST be rejected.
Businesses MUST enforce exact string matching for the redirect_uri parameter during authorization requests; the token-request redirect_uri MUST be identical to the authorization-request one.
For loopback redirect URIs (127.0.0.1 or [::1]) businesses MUST ignore the port component and match on scheme, host, and path only.
Businesses MUST declare supported client authentication methods in token_endpoint_auth_methods_supported and enforce one of the declared methods at the token endpoint.
When 'none' is advertised, businesses MUST require PKCE with S256 and MUST reject any authorization code redemption lacking a valid code_verifier.
Requests that fail the negotiated authentication method MUST be rejected with invalid_client; requests that fail PKCE MUST be rejected with invalid_grant.
Businesses MUST validate user identity tokens on every user-authenticated request (iss, aud, exp, scopes, and client_id/azp).
Businesses MUST emit a WWW-Authenticate: Bearer challenge on 401 Unauthorized (identity_required) and 403 Forbidden (insufficient_scope) responses to user-authenticated operations.
Businesses MUST implement token revocation; revoking a refresh_token MUST also immediately invalidate all access_tokens issued from it.
Businesses MUST support revocation requests authenticated with the same client credentials used at the token endpoint.
Businesses MUST support standard UCP scopes as defined in the Scopes section.
On RFC 8414 discovery, any non-2xx response other than 404 (network error or timeout) MUST abort and MUST NOT proceed to OIDC fallback.
Platforms MUST NOT silently fall through on any error other than 404 in RFC 8414 discovery.
The issuer value in discovered metadata MUST match the discovery base URI exactly; platforms MUST NOT normalize before comparison (byte-for-byte match).
Platforms MUST request only the derived scope set (from config.scopes) and not a superset.
On the authorization response, the platform MUST verify state matches the sent value and iss matches the discovered issuer URI; if either fails it MUST discard the response.
The token-request code_verifier MUST correspond to the code_challenge sent in the authorization request; businesses MUST reject token requests where code_verifier is absent or does not verify.
Scope names MUST match the pattern ^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$.
Platforms MUST treat any scope listed in config.scopes (well-known or custom) as gating its operations behind user authentication.
Platforms MUST ignore unrecognized fields in a per-scope policy object.
On identity_required the business MUST return 401 Unauthorized, a WWW-Authenticate: Bearer challenge, and a UCP error body with code 'identity_required'.
The identity_required WWW-Authenticate header MUST include a realm parameter set to the business's issuer URI from RFC 8414 metadata.
When the request included a token that was invalid/expired/unverifiable, the identity_required header MUST also include error="invalid_token".
continue_url MUST NOT be used to convey a pre-baked OAuth authorization request; the platform constructs its own authorization request.
On insufficient_scope the business MUST return 403 Forbidden, a WWW-Authenticate: Bearer challenge, and a UCP error body with code 'insufficient_scope'.
The insufficient_scope WWW-Authenticate header MUST include realm, error="insufficient_scope", and scope="<space-separated full required scope set>".
The insufficient_scope scope parameter MUST list the full set of scopes required for the operation, not just the missing ones.
Platforms MUST NOT retry an insufficient_scope response by re-initiating a fresh account linking flow; they MUST instead request only the missing scope(s) via incremental authorization.
Plain PKCE (plain) MUST NOT be used; PKCE S256 is REQUIRED for all authorization code flows, and businesses MUST reject exchanges without a valid code_verifier.
Public clients cannot keep a client_secret confidential and MUST use 'none'; businesses MUST NOT require client_secret_basic as the only method when serving native or agent platforms.
Platforms MUST process the structured scope and error parameters to drive authorization flow decisions; error_description MUST NOT be used for control-flow decisions.
The realm parameter MUST match the business's issuer URI so platforms can correlate the challenge with the correct authorization server.
All communication between platform and business MUST use HTTPS with a minimum of TLS 1.2.
Platforms MUST revoke user identity tokens at the business's revocation endpoint when a user unlinks; businesses MUST reject subsequent requests that present revoked tokens.
When config.providers is absent (v1), platforms MUST fall back to direct OAuth 2.0 against the business domain via RFC 8414 discovery.
When config contains fields not defined in this version, platforms MUST ignore those fields and proceed using OAuth 2.0 with RFC 8414 discovery on the business domain.
authorization_response_iss_parameter_supported and code_challenge_methods_supported:["S256"] MUST both be present in UCP-compliant RFC 8414 metadata.
config.scopes property names MUST conform to the scope_token pattern {capability}:{scope}.
Business identity_linking config object is required and MUST contain a 'scopes' property.
When the platform's protocol version is unsupported, the business MUST return a version_unsupported error, mapped to HTTP 422 (REST).
When no compatible capabilities exist in the intersection, the business MUST return a capabilities_incompatible error, mapped to HTTP 200 with the error in the UCP body (NOT a 4xx).
A resolved-but-unfetchable profile (timeout/non-2xx) maps to profile_unreachable, HTTP 424.
A fetched profile that is not valid JSON or violates schema maps to profile_malformed, HTTP 422.
Businesses MUST return the full order entity (current-state snapshot) on every response, not deltas
Line items MUST include all line items that ever existed on the order regardless of edits or alterations
order.currency is REQUIRED on the order entity
line_items is REQUIRED on the order entity
Each order line item requires id, item, quantity, totals, and status
Line item quantity requires total and fulfilled (non-negative integers)
Adjustment quantities and amounts are signed — negative for reductions (returns, refunds), positive for additions (exchanges)
Each adjustment requires id, type, occurred_at, and status
Adjustment totals are signed — negative for money returned to buyer (refunds, credits), positive for additional charges (exchanges)
The business MUST authenticate requests to order data before returning a response (API key, OAuth 2.0, mutual TLS, or HTTP Message Signatures)
Platforms MUST include the UCP-Agent header with profile URL on all order requests
Each expectation requires id, line_items, method_type, and destination
Each fulfillment event requires id, occurred_at, type, and line_items
Order confirmation requires id and permalink_url
All REST response bodies MUST be valid JSON (RFC 8259)
All REST endpoints MUST be served over HTTPS with minimum TLS version 1.3
Platforms MUST check the messages array in responses before accessing order data
Webhook payloads MUST be signed by the business and verified by the platform
Businesses MUST sign all webhook payloads per Message Signatures using RFC 9421 headers (Signature, Signature-Input, Content-Digest)
Businesses MUST include the UCP-Agent header with profile URL for signer identification on webhooks
Businesses MUST send the 'Order created' event with a fully populated order entity
Businesses MUST send the full order entity on updates (not incremental deltas)
Businesses MUST retry failed webhook deliveries
Platforms MUST respond quickly with a 2xx HTTP status code to acknowledge webhook receipt
After verifying the signature, the platform MUST confirm the signer is authorized to send events for the referenced order (profile matches order's business; reject otherwise)
A business/platform/response profile advertises payment handlers in the payment_handlers registry, keyed by reverse-domain name
Every payment handler declaration MUST include an id (handler instance identifier)
Checkout/Order responses echo handlers in ucp.payment_handlers; the response payment_handlers array is required and each entry conforms to the handler response_schema (which requires id)
A platform-schema payment handler declaration MUST additionally include spec and schema URLs
When a handler declares available_instruments, the array MUST contain at least one instrument type (minItems 1)
An available_payment_instrument MUST include a type (instrument type identifier)
Platforms MUST treat the response_schema available_instruments / handler config as authoritative for the checkout
The available_instruments returned in the response_schema is authoritative; platforms MUST treat it as authoritative and MUST NOT use instrument types or constraints that contradict it
Platforms MUST treat the available_instruments in the checkout response as authoritative for that checkout
Credentials flow Platform -> Business only; businesses MUST NOT echo credentials back in responses
Token credential value MUST NOT be echoed in responses (token field is omit-on-response)
Businesses MUST validate handler_id before processing (ensure handler is in the advertised set)
Businesses MUST dynamically filter the handlers list based on the context of the cart (e.g., removing BNPL for subscription items, filtering regional methods by shipping address)
On 3DS/SCA soft-decline the business returns status requires_escalation with a continue_url; the platform MUST open continue_url for the buyer to complete the bank check then retry completion
continue_url MUST be provided when checkout status is requires_escalation
A payment instrument MUST include id, handler_id, and type
A payment credential MUST include a type discriminator
A card credential MUST include type and card_number_type
Card credential (raw PAN) MUST NOT be used for checkout, only with payment handlers that tokenize or encrypt credentials
A card payment instrument MUST have type const 'card'
A token credential MUST include type and token
If using token credentials, the handler schema MUST include an expiration field (expiry, ttl, or similar) so platforms know when to refresh credentials
A token binding MUST include the checkout_id the token is bound to (prevents token reuse across checkouts/participants)
A payment identity MUST include an access_token uniquely identifying the participant tokens are bound to
Every payment handler specification MUST define the core elements (identity, participants, prerequisites, declaration, instrument, credential, acquisition, processing, error mapping)
A payment handler specification MUST define which credential types are accepted by the business
Sensitive data such as Payment Credentials or PII MUST be handled according to PCI-DSS and GDPR guidelines
Signal values MUST NOT be buyer-asserted claims; platforms provide signals from direct observation or independently verifiable third-party attestations
All signal keys MUST use reverse-domain naming to ensure provenance and prevent collisions in the shared namespace
Signal keys must match the reverse-domain identifier pattern (well-known dev.ucp namespace; extensions use their own)
Numeric or boolean attribution values MUST be string-encoded as they would be in a URL query string
The presence or absence of the attribution field MUST NOT affect the response or negotiation
Eligibility values MUST use reverse-domain naming and MUST be non-identifying
Businesses MUST ignore unrecognized eligibility values without error
Eligibility and policy enforcement MUST occur at checkout time using binding transaction data (context is non-authoritative)
All accepted eligibility claims MUST be resolved before the transaction can complete
Unrecognized or inapplicable eligibility claims MUST NOT block the checkout
At completion, accepted claims that remain unverified MUST result in type 'error' with code 'eligibility_invalid'
Businesses MUST NOT complete a transaction with unresolved eligibility claims
Eligibility verification failure MUST only affect the messages array
On eligibility verification failure the Business MUST return an error in messages with code 'eligibility_invalid' and severity 'recoverable'
UCP signs and verifies HTTP messages using RFC 9421 HTTP Message Signatures across all HTTP-based transports.
All implementations MUST support verifying P-256 (ES256) signatures.
The algorithm is derived from the key's crv field in the JWK; alg is NOT included in Signature-Input parameters.
Public keys MUST be represented using JSON Web Key (JWK) format as defined in RFC 7517.
Public keys are published in the signing_keys array of the party's UCP profile at /.well-known/ucp.
During key rotation, continue accepting signatures from old keys for a grace period of minimum 7 days.
Content-Digest follows RFC 9530 and hashes the raw body bytes; implementations MUST use sha-256.
Proxies, API gateways, and other intermediaries MUST NOT re-serialize JSON bodies, as this would invalidate the signature.
Content-Digest header is required when a request/response has a body.
Signature-Input and Signature headers are required on requests and responses.
REST request signed components MUST include @method, @authority, and @path (all Required).
REST request signed components MUST include content-digest and content-type when the request has a body.
idempotency-key MUST be a signed component for POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH requests.
@query MUST be a signed component if the request has query parameters.
ucp-agent MUST be a signed component if the UCP-Agent header is present.
ECDSA signatures MUST use fixed-width raw r||s encoding per RFC 9421, not ASN.1/DER (64 bytes P-256, 96 bytes P-384).
REST response signed components use @status instead of @method (Required).
REST response signed components MUST include content-digest and content-type if the response has a body.
Idempotency-Key must have minimum 128 bits of entropy (e.g., UUID v4, 22+ char alphanumeric).
Server storage of idempotency keys must be a minimum of 24 hours, recommended 48 hours.
On idempotency storage failure, fail closed (reject request with 503).
On duplicate idempotency key, return cached response and do not re-execute.
Webhook notifications MUST be signed.
signature_missing maps to HTTP 401 (required signature header/field not present).
signature_invalid maps to HTTP 401 (signature verification failed).
key_not_found maps to HTTP 401 (key ID not found in signer's signing_keys).
digest_mismatch maps to HTTP 400 (body digest doesn't match Content-Digest header).
algorithm_unsupported maps to HTTP 400 (signature algorithm not supported).
Verification must reconstruct the signature base and reject with signature_invalid if ECDSA verification fails.
Verification must return key_not_found when the kid is not present in the signer's signing_keys.
Verification must return digest_mismatch when Content-Digest does not match the recomputed sha-256 of the body bytes.
Platforms MUST render all top-level totals entries in the order provided
Platforms MUST NOT interpret, filter, reorder, aggregate, or apply their own display logic to totals
Unknown (non-well-known) totals types MUST include display_text
Exactly one totals entry of type subtotal MUST be present
Exactly one totals entry of type total MUST be present
Platforms MUST NOT substitute their own computed totals for the business values
Platforms MUST NOT autonomously complete a checkout with mismatched totals
When display_text is provided, platforms MUST use it
Subtractive well-known types (discount, items_discount) MUST have negative amounts
Additive well-known types (subtotal, fulfillment, tax, fee) MUST have non-negative amounts
Sum of a parent entry lines[] amounts MUST equal the parent entry amount
Each sub-line entry requires both display_text and amount
Each top-level totals entry requires both type and amount
Price requires a non-negative integer amount and an ISO 4217 currency code
